institution: University of Colorado Denver/Anschutz Medical Campus

ai_strategy.summary: University of Colorado Denver/Anschutz Medical Campus's public AI posture combines published guidance, teaching resources, research centers, AI-relevant programs, and research computing. Public evidence points to distributed governance rather than a verified standalone chief AI officer.

key_provisions: Use appropriate or approved AI tools within institutional data rules
Faculty should set clear course-level AI expectations
Users remain responsible for accuracy, attribution, privacy, and academic integrity
Sensitive data should not be entered into unapproved public tools
ai_policy.summary: CU Denver and Anschutz publish generative AI technology and teaching guidance, with CU system digital education resources adding system-level support.

governance_structure.summary: University of Colorado Denver/Anschutz Medical Campus's AI governance is distributed across IT, teaching support, and research units.

regulatory_context.summary: University of Colorado Denver/Anschutz Medical Campus operates under federal education, privacy, and research obligations plus University of Colorado system rules. This sweep found institutional AI guidance but no separate campus-specific AI statute.

narrative_summary: University of Colorado Denver/Anschutz Medical Campus is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is visible in responsible-use guidance, classroom resources, AI-relevant research structures, credentials or workforce programming, and research computing. The campus permits AI experimentation with guardrails rather than presenting a simple prohibition. Faculty-facing materials make course policy and assessment design central to implementation, while technology guidance emphasizes privacy, security, attribution, and responsible use. Research evidence comes from AI, machine-learning, data-science, policy, health, autonomy, or computing organizations, supported by high-performance or research computing resources. The profile is strongest on public policy resources, research centers, programs, and infrastructure, and weaker on exact budgets, enterprise license counts, adoption metrics, and named executive ownership. Overall, the institution appears to be in a growing-to-operational phase: AI work is real and distributed across functions, but public evidence does not yet show a single centralized AI operating model with disclosed outcomes.

gaps_acknowledged: Current president, provost, CIO, and chief AI officer were not independently verified in this worker sweep, so leadership names are left null.
Exact enterprise AI contract values, user counts, and adoption metrics were not publicly confirmed.
AI-specific total budget was not disclosed in the public sources used.
No institution-specific AI litigation or major controversy was confirmed.

Follow-up search closed compute_capacity at the named-system level with Alpine HPC and closed platform_usage_or_contracts at product/scope level, but no CU Anschutz AI tool user counts, contract values, or Anschutz-specific GPU allocation counts were public.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
